name: Mass Spectrometry
description: Mass spectrometry ionizes a sample to molecular and fragment ions, accelerates them into a mass analyzer that separates by mass-to-charge ratio, and detects abundances; identifying the molecular ion and interpreting fragmentation reveals mass and structure.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Sample introduced"]
    N2["Ionization (EI / ESI)"]
    N3["Molecular ion + fragments"]
    N4["Accelerate into analyzer"]
    N5["Separate by m/z"]
    N6["Detect ion abundance"]
    N7{"Molecular ion clear?"}
    N8["Use soft ionization"]
    N9["Interpret fragmentation"]
    N10["Structure / mass"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 -->|no| N8
    N8 -->|re-ionize| N2
    N7 -->|yes| N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Electrospray ionization for mass spectrometry of large biomolecules
sources.authors: Fenn, J. B.; Mann, M.; Meng, C. K.; Wong, S. F.; Whitehouse, C. M.

sources.title: Mass Spectrometry: Principles and Applications, 3rd ed.
Principles of Instrumental Analysis, 7th ed. — Molecular Mass Spectrometry
sources.authors: Skoog, D. A.; Holler, F. J.; Crouch, S. R.
